title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: folder segmented minirhizotron images want depth-resolved root traits one call — without writing loops — root_depth_metrics() right starting point. handles file loading, depth-map construction, per-bin zonal statistics, derived metrics, fault tolerance, optional file output. toggle metric groups compute; everything else taken care . line--line explanation underlying steps, see Minirhizotron Scans vignette.

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: Images three directories must alphabetical order ; position corresponds physical tube/window.

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jcunow/RootScanR")  library(RootScanR) library(tidyverse)   # for downstream plotting

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: fastest way get going. path.seg required; everything else sensible defaults. path.skl optional — omitted, skeletons needed length, diameter, angle metrics computed internally via skeletonize_image(). result data frame one row per tube × depth-bin combination. Always-present columns: Tube, Session, Plot, depth. Default metric columns: rootpx, voidpx, rootlength, avg.diameter, max.diameter, var.diameter, rootpx.density, rootlength.density, mrd, rpi, total.length.density, rootlength.fraction, mean.var.diameter, rootsurface_rootvolume_ratio.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   # optional — computed internally if omitted   session  = "2022_02" )  head(result)

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: tubes differ insertion angle soil-surface position, supply vector values (one per image). accept single recycled value images share setting. tube names follow different convention default (characters 3–5 right filename), always supply tube_names explicitly.
code: # Load your field metadata table tube_meta <- read.csv("metadata/tube_metadata_2022_02.csv") # Expected columns: filename, angle, soil_row, tube_id  result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg         = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl         = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   insertion_angles = tube_meta$angle,       # degrees from vertical   soil_starts      = tube_meta$soil_row,    # pixel row of soil surface   tube_names       = tube_meta$tube_id,     # e.g. "T042"   session          = "2022_02",   dpi              = 300 )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: metric group logical argument. Disabled groups produce NA columns rather absent columns, downstream code can always reference column names regardless computed.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg  = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl  = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   path.rgb  = "scans/blended/2022_02/",   session   = "2022_02",    # --- core (on by default, shown explicitly for clarity) ---   calc_root_pixels    = TRUE,   calc_root_length    = TRUE,   calc_diameter_stats = TRUE,    # --- derived (on by default) ---   calc_density_metrics      = TRUE,   calc_distribution_indices = TRUE,   calc_advanced_metrics     = TRUE,    # --- extended (off by default — enable as needed) ---   calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE,   # 90th/95th/99th percentile + modal peaks   calc_color_metrics      = TRUE,   # RGB colour per root vs background pixels   calc_root_angles        = TRUE,   # deep_drive + steepness angle distribution   calc_root_order_metrics = TRUE,   # branching order (main vs lateral roots)   calc_landscape_metrics  = FALSE   # slow — enable only when needed )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE, can specify custom diameter thresholds separate fine roots coarse roots:
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg                = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl                = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE,   diameter_thresholds     = c(0.2, 0.5, 1.0),   # fine / medium / coarse   diameter_threshold_unit = "mm"                 # "mm", "cm", or "px" ) # Resulting columns include: rootlength.above.0.2mm, rootlength.above.0.5mm, ...

what: Root branching order (main vs. lateral roots)
title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: calc_root_order_metrics = TRUE builds segment graph skeleton image (via branch_order_map()) classifies every root segment branch order — thickest, central root connected component order 1 (“main” axis), laterals order 2, laterals order 3, . See Minirhizotron Scans vignette worked example underlying pipeline. adds two kinds columns: Per depth bin — branching order varies depth: Per tube (repeated every row tube) — main root vs. lateral roots, split order_metrics(..., focal = "thickest"): Slow. builds one segment graph per image, considerably expensive metric groups. Disable (default) quick exploratory runs enable need architectural detail.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg                = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl                = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   session                 = "2022_02",   calc_root_order_metrics = TRUE ) library(ggplot2)  ggplot(result, aes(x = depth, y = lateral_root_fraction, colour = Tube)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   coord_flip() +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Lateral Root Fraction by Depth",     x     = "Soil Depth (cm)",     y     = "Fraction of skeleton pixels with branch order > 1"   )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: glass-fronted rhizotron boxes (flat geometry, tube curvature), set flat_geometry = TRUE. disables sinusoidal depth correction otherwise applied cylindrical minirhizotron tubes.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg      = "scans/segmented/rhizotron_A/",   path.skl      = "scans/skeleton/rhizotron_A/",   flat_geometry = TRUE,   insertion_angles = 0   # vertical windows )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: skeleton RGB directories contain files segmented directory (e.g. multiple sessions one folder), use *_file_index select matching subset:
code: # Use only files 37–72 from the RGB directory result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg       = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl       = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   path.rgb       = "scans/blended/all_sessions/",   rgb_file_index = 37:72,   session        = "2022_02" )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: Pass output_path save result .RData file (object named root.depth.metrics inside file). directory created exist.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg    = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl    = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   session     = "2022_02",   output_path = "output/root_metrics_2022_02.RData" )  # Reload later: # load("output/root_metrics_2022_02.RData") # head(root.depth.metrics)

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: default (verbose = TRUE) function prints progress line image showing per-image time, cumulative elapsed time, estimated remaining time, predicted clock-time completion: metric block fails single image (e.g. corrupted skeleton file), skipped [RootScanR] SKIPPED message corresponding columns filled NA. Processing always continues. Images loaded skipped entirely listed warning end. suppress progress output:
code: [RootScanR] [3/48] T042 | img: 12s | elapsed: 38s | remaining: ~546s | done ~14:22 result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   verbose  = FALSE )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
code: library(ggplot2)  # Root length density by depth, one line per tube ggplot(result, aes(x = depth, y = rootlength.density, colour = Tube)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   coord_flip() +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Root Length Density by Depth",     x     = "Soil Depth (cm)",     y     = "Root Length Density (cm root / cm² area)"   )  # Average root diameter by depth ggplot(result, aes(x = depth, y = avg.diameter)) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE) +   coord_flip() +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Average Root Diameter with Depth",     x     = "Soil Depth (cm)",     y     = "Average Diameter (cm)"   )  # mrd distribution across tubes ggplot(result |> dplyr::distinct(Tube, mrd), aes(x = mrd)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 15, fill = "steelblue4", colour = "white") +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Mean Root Depth Distribution (MRD)",     x     = "MRD",     y     = "Count"   )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
code: tube_meta <- read.csv("metadata/tube_metadata_2022_02.csv")  result <- root_depth_metrics(    # Paths   path.seg       = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl       = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   path.rgb       = "scans/blended/2022_02/",    # Per-image metadata   insertion_angles = tube_meta$angle,   soil_starts      = tube_meta$soil_row,   tube_names       = tube_meta$tube_id,   session          = "2022_02",    # Geometry   dpi               = 300,   tube_diameter_cm  = 7,   depth_interval_cm = 5,   flat_geometry     = FALSE,    # Core metrics   calc_root_pixels    = TRUE,   calc_root_length    = TRUE,   calc_diameter_stats = TRUE,    # Extended metrics   calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE,   calc_color_metrics      = TRUE,   calc_root_angles        = TRUE,   calc_root_order_metrics = TRUE,    # main vs. lateral root architecture   calc_landscape_metrics  = FALSE,   # enable if you need patch metrics    # Derived metrics   calc_density_metrics      = TRUE,   calc_distribution_indices = TRUE,   calc_advanced_metrics     = TRUE,    # Diameter thresholds (for quantile block)   diameter_thresholds     = c(0.2, 0.5, 1.0),   diameter_threshold_unit = "mm",    # Output   output_path = "output/root_metrics_2022_02.RData",   verbose     = TRUE )

title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: Flatbed scanning captures root morphology 2D plane high resolution controlled lighting. Unlike minirhizotron images, flatbed scans depth dimension — full root system visible traits computed globally rather per depth bin. vignette covers individual-function workflow flatbed images. working minirhizotron tubes want single-call batch approach, see Batch Processing vignette instead. Prerequisite: RootScanR works already-segmented images (root = 1, background = 0). Segmentation must done beforehand using RootDetector RootPainter.

previous_headings: Analyzing Root Systems from Flatbed Scans
title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
code: # install.packages("remotes") # remotes::install_github("jcunow/RootScanR")  library(RootScanR) library(terra) library(tidyverse)

previous_headings: Analyzing Root Systems from Flatbed Scans > Workflow
title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: load_flexible_image() accepts rasters, arrays, file paths, common image formats. Set binarize = TRUE force image 0/1 values.
code: # Segmented image (binary: root = 1, background = 0) seg <- load_flexible_image(   "path/to/segmented_scan.tif",   output_format = "spatrast",   normalize     = TRUE,   binarize      = TRUE,   select.layer  = 2        # RootDetector: layer 2 is the root channel )  # Original RGB scan for colour analysis rgb <- load_flexible_image(   "path/to/rgb_scan.tif",   output_format = "spatrast",   normalize     = FALSE )  plot(seg, main = "Segmented scan") plot(rgb, main = "RGB scan")

previous_headings: Analyzing Root Systems from Flatbed Scans > Workflow
title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: skeletonize_image() reduces roots single-pixel-wide centrelines. required root length (Kimura method) diameter estimation. skeletonize_image() uses LUT-based Zhang-Suen thinning algorithm reduce segmented mask one-pixel-wide centrelines.
code: skl <- skeletonize_image(   seg,   select.layer = 2,   verbose      = TRUE )  plot(skl, main = "Skeleton")

previous_headings: Analyzing Root Systems from Flatbed Scans > Workflow
title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: root_length() uses Kimura’s formula, weights orthogonal diagonal skeleton segments differently approximate true path length.

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: vignette demonstrates detect correct rotational bias minirhizotron setups using RootScanR package. minirhizotron studies, scanner tube inserted angle soil can rotate slightly sessions. CI-600 similar scanners cover full 360° arc, sequential images tube may perfectly overlap. Left uncorrected, rotation introduces systematic spatial bias — roots near scan edges observed sessions others. RootScanR addresses four functions: estimate_rotation_center() — locates rotational zero point tape coverage single image estimate_rotation_shift() — quantifies pixel offset two sessions using image correlation rotation_censor() — crops images shared, overlap region zoning(mode = "rotation") — splits tube surface slices along rotation axis (circumference), root traits can summarised separately slice last point matters beyond rotation correction : tube circumference split slices, rhythmicity() / fit_sine_curve() family — normally introduced time-series data — can applied sequence per-slice root traits test whether roots distributed evenly around tube circumference, whether systematic top-/ side--side pattern (e.g. roots underside tube). spatial, temporal, application functions — see Circumferential zoning rhythmicity section .

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
code: # install.packages("remotes") # remotes::install_github("jcunow/RootScanR")  library(RootScanR) library(terra) ## terra 1.9.27

what: 1. Estimate the rotational center from a single image
title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: estimate_rotation_center() detects white adhesive tape attached upper side tube. tape visible one side, function infers top tube (rotational zero) lies. returns pixel row index. Since tube fixed geometry, needs done per tube — rotational center change sessions. Key parameters:
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) img <- terra::rast(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067)  r0 <- estimate_rotation_center(img) print(paste("Estimated rotation center (row):", r0))

what: 2. Estimate the rotation shift between two sessions
title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: tube scanned two different sessions, scanner may rotated. estimate_rotation_shift() uses either cross-correlation ("ccf") phase correlation ("phase") shared depth window find pixel offset. Phase correlation generally robust brightness differences sessions.  returned vector c(x.lag, y.lag) — horizontal (depth axis) vertical (rotation axis) pixel shifts. large vertical shift means tube rotated substantially sessions. Planned: single helper estimates estimate_rotation_shift() two sessions applies rotation_censor() using shift directly, aligning session coverage becomes one-step operation. , use two functions shown steps 2 3.
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067)  shift <- estimate_rotation_shift(   seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067,   seg_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067,   cor.type          = "phase",   fixed.depth.pixel = c(1000, 4000) ) ## Warning in doTryCatch(return(expr), name, parentenv, handler): Image size ## mismatch detected; cropping to common extent cat("Rotation shift (depth px, rotation px): ", shift[1:2], "\n") ## Rotation shift (depth px, rotation px):  -18 -9 # Visual inspection estimate_rotation_shift(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067, seg_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067, cor.type = "phase", select.layer = 2, overlay = T) ## Warning in doTryCatch(return(expr), name, parentenv, handler): Image size ## mismatch detected; cropping to common extent

what: 3. Censor image edges to the shared overlap region
title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: rotation_censor() crops image rows present every session retained. eliminates non-overlapping margins makes root counts directly comparable across sessions. Two modes available: fixed.rotation = FALSE — cuts proportionally based measured offset; output width varies fixed.rotation = TRUE — centres crop specified row forces fixed output width; recommended comparing multiple sessions Note tube geometry. inner outer tube diameters differ, observed root length slightly underestimates true root length soil. resize coefficient may applied separately; rotation_censor() handle correction.
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) img <- terra::rast(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067)  # rotation centre (absolute row) to centre the crop on r0 <- estimate_rotation_center(img)  # preview the crop (green = kept, red = cut) and apply it in one call. # fixed.width must be <= image height (1144 rows here) to sit symmetrically. censored <- rotation_censor(   img,   center.offset  = r0,   cut.buffer     = 0.02,   fixed.width    = 800,   fixed.rotation = TRUE,   overlay        = TRUE,   main = "Rotation censor: kept window (green) vs cut (red)" )

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: minirhizotron image long, narrow strip represents slice tube’s circumference, depth running along one axis rotation (circumferential) position running along . zoning(mode = "rotation") divides image along rotation axis rotation_total_slices equal slices, rotation_slices = c(, ) extracts single slice . Looping slices gives sequence root-trait values indexed circumferential position. rhythmicity() / fit_sine_curve() functions — fit y=Asin⁡(2πP(x+ϕ))+cy = \sin\!\left(\frac{2\pi}{P}(x + \phi)\right) + c test whether ≠0A \neq 0 — agnostic x represents. Applied sequence x = slice index period P fixed rotation_total_slices (one full turn around tube), test whether roots distributed evenly around tube concentrated one side (e.g. systematic top-bias gravitropism, light incidence one face installation, installation-angle artefact).

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067)  root_layer <- load_flexible_image(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067, normalize = TRUE, binarize = TRUE, select.layer = 2,                                   output_format = "spatrast")   # rotation trim: drop the top 33% of rows e <- terra::ext(root_layer) root_layer <- terra::crop(root_layer, terra::ext(e[1]+ e[2] / 4, e[2], e[3], e[4]))  plot(root_layer) # --> the trimming avoids noisy deep layer and tape artefacts at the top  # create circumferential zones n_slices <- 48 slices <- slice_rotation(root_layer, n_slices)  # tallies from the binary mask: roots = 1, soil/background = 0 n_root <- function(s) { v <- terra::values(s, mat = FALSE); sum(v > 0,  na.rm = TRUE) } n_soil <- function(s) { v <- terra::values(s, mat = FALSE); sum(v == 0, na.rm = TRUE) }  slice_traits <- data.frame(   slice   = seq_len(n_slices),   rootlen = sapply(slices, root_length, unit = "cm", dpi = 300),  # cm   rootpx  = sapply(slices, n_root),   soilpx  = sapply(slices, n_soil) )  # root length per observed area (root + soil pixels) slice_traits$rld <- slice_traits$rootlen /                     ((slice_traits$rootpx + slice_traits$soilpx) * (2.54/300)^2) #slice_traits$rld.fraction <- slice_traits$rld / sum(slice_traits$rld, na.rm = TRUE)

Fitting rhythmicity() separately within depth bin (x = slice, y = trait per slice) tests whether circumferential pattern consistent depth changes shallow deep — .e. genuine top-amplitude gradient.
code: slice_seq <- seq(1, n_slices, length.out = 200) fit_seq   <- res$amplitude *               sin(2 * pi / n_slices * (slice_seq + res$phase)) +               res$offset  plot(slice_traits$slice, slice_traits$rld, pch = 16, col = "grey50",      xlab = "Circumferential slice (1 = top, going around the tube)",      ylab = "Root Length Density (cm / cm2)",      main = "Root distribution around tube circumference") lines(slice_seq, fit_seq, col = "coral", lwd = 2) abline( a = res$offset, b = 0, col  = "grey40", lty = 3)  segments(x0 = res$peakTime,          x1 = res$peakTime,          y0 = res$offset,          y1 = res$offset + res$amplitude,          lwd = 2, lty = 2,          col = "grey40") segments(x0 = res$peakTime + res$period/2,          x1 = res$peakTime +  res$period/2,          y0 = res$offset,          y1 = res$offset -  res$amplitude,          lwd = 2, lty = 2,          col = "grey40") legend("topright",        legend = sprintf("A = %.4f, R² = %.3f, p = %.4f",                         res$amplitude, res$R2, res$p_value),        bty = "n")   # cross section view library(ggplot2) inner <- 6 ggplot(slice_traits, aes(slice, rld + inner)) +     geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +     coord_polar(start = pi) +   xlab("") + ylab ("")+ ggtitle("Circumferential Root Length Distribution")+   theme_light()+   theme(axis.text = element_blank()) +    annotate("rect",            xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,            ymin = 0, ymax = inner,            fill = "white", color = NA)

title: Special Topics: Peat Colour, Soil Texture, Rhizosphere Halos, and Turnover
text: create_root_buffer() grows buffer chosen width around every root pixel – simple proxy rhizosphere / diffusion zone. halo.= TRUE returns ring around roots (excluding roots ).  kernel argument switches "circle" (8-neighbour) "diamond" (4-neighbour) growth shape, width controls many dilation iterations applied. Set halo.= FALSE return roots plus buffer single filled mask.
code: seg  <- terra::rast(seg_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067) halo <- create_root_buffer(seg, width = 3, halo.only = TRUE, kernel = "circle") terra::plot(halo)

title: Special Topics: Peat Colour, Soil Texture, Rhizosphere Halos, and Turnover
text: root_turnover() quantifies much root material produced, lost, retained. method selects one two approaches: "tc" (Temporal Comparison) – compare two segmented images different sessions. tc.method argument picks root amount measured: "kimura" (root length) "rootpx" (root pixel count). "dpc" (Decay, Production, Constant) – decompose single multi-layer image produced RootDetector decayed, newly produced, unchanged root fractions. bundled TurnoverDPC_data DPC-style image: two-timepoint comparison instead pass sessions:
code: dpc <- terra::rast(TurnoverDPC_data) root_turnover(dpc, method = "dpc") #>       tape constant production    decay newgrowth.ratio decay.ratio #> 1 3478.478 1720.318   10863.29 13159.69          0.8633      0.8844 #>   constant.ratio #> 1         0.0668 s1 <- terra::rast(skl_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) s3 <- terra::rast(skl_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067) root_turnover(s1, s3, method = "tc", tc.method = "kimura", unit = "cm", dpi = 300)

Start batch-processing tutorial — runs whole pipeline (loading, depth mapping, trait extraction) folder images single function call. step--step tutorials need full control individual processing stage. 🚀 Start : Batch Processing 📄 Minirhizotron Workflow (step--step) 📄 Flatbed Scan Workflow (step--step) 📄 Rotation Bias Correction tutorial includes code, images, overlays, tips interpretation.

title: RootScanR: Extract Root Traits from Images
code: # Load example flatbed scan library(RootScanR) library(terra) data("seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067") img <- seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067  # Preprocess and extract traits binary <- load_flexible_image(img) cleaned <- clean_image(binary, max_artifact_size = 5, max_hole_size = 5, select.layer = 2) skel <- skeletonize_image(cleaned,  methods = "GuoHall", verbose = F)  # Length and diameter root_length(skel, unit = "cm", dpi = 150) diam_map <- root_diameter(cleaned, unit = "cm", dpi = 150, select.layer = NULL ) modal_peaks(diam_map$diameters, prominence_threshold = 10, mclust = F, adjust = 5, display_type = "density")   # Architecture metrics <- detect_skeleton_points(skel) print(metrics)  # Branch order: main axis (order 1) vs. lateral roots (order >= 2) order_res <- branch_order_map(skel, mask = cleaned, order = "branch_order", unit = "cm", dpi = 150) order_metrics(order_res, focal = "thickest")  # Visualize terra::plot(diam_map$diameter_rast) terra::plot(skel)

title: Branch-order classification of a root skeleton — branch_order_map
text: skel Binary skeleton: single-layer SpatRaster (preferred) 0/1 matrix. Pass raster directly; pre-convert .matrix(). NULL, computed mask via skeletonize_image(). mask Filled (un-thinned) root mask grid, used distance transform / diameters. NULL, skeleton used diameters collapse ~1 px. Required skel NULL. order scheme labels $class_map/$summary: "branch_order", "root_order", "tip_order". unit Reporting unit: "cm", "inch", "px". dpi Scan resolution (dots per inch); required cm/inch. length_method "polyline" (sqrt(2) chain code, follows curves) "kimura" (per-segment Kimura correction, better straight segments). template SpatRaster defining $class_map grid; defaults skel raster. overlay_png Optional path; writes order-coloured validation PNG. return_map Build $class_map. ... Passed root_graph_pipeline (e.g. dt_backend, crossing_straight, prune_iter, diam_weight — diameter-vs-angle weight continuation rule, >= 0).

title: Branch-order classification of a root skeleton — branch_order_map
text: object class "branchOrderMap": list $edges,   $summary, $class_map (SpatRaster; chosen order per pixel,   NA -root), $order, $unit, $dpi,   $length_method.

title: Branch-order classification of a root skeleton — branch_order_map
text: three order schemes always computed stored $edges; order selects labels $class_map $summary. tip_order (per segment) Topological leaf-peeling (Strahler-like).     Every terminal segment order 1; peeling terminals away round round,     segment's order 1 + max(child orders). Order rises toward     interior, distal end even thick root 1. root_order (per root) Segments grouped continuous roots     (continuation rule ); root takes maximum tip_order     along , thick main axis keeps high order tip. Sensitive     deep deepest subtree runs. branch_order (per root) Centrifugal generation. thickest root     (length-weighted mean diameter) connected component order 1;     counting branching hops outward, laterals 2, 3, .     Independent subtree depth, one heavily branched root inflate     rest. Continuation rule (groups segments roots root_order branch_order): junction two arms forming root chosen straightness + diam_weight * diameter_similarity. diam_weight = 0 uses angle ; larger values let thickness dominate. odd arm lateral. tip_order use rule.

title: Build peat class centroids from manual RGB colour picks — build_peat_centroids
text: picks named list RGB pick matrices. element corresponds one class must numeric matrix 3 columns (R, G, B), values 0-255, one row per pick. Names become class names output. See classify_peat_rgb's Building centroids (picks) section worked example constructing matrices image (e.g. cropping representative patches calling terra::values()). max_dist named numeric vector per-class LAB distance thresholds, matched name picks. Pixels class centroid assigned class. prior Optional data.frame existing centroids (format output function, .default_peat_centroids()). supplied, new centroids derived picks blended prior centroids using alpha. classes present picks prior blended; new classes picks absent prior added -. alpha Numeric [0, 1]. Blend weight prior centroids. alpha = 0 (default) ignores prior entirely – centroids derived purely new picks. alpha = 1 returns prior unchanged. alpha = 0.5 weights old new equally. Decrease alpha progressively collect new picks gradually shift calibration toward new dataset. verbose Logical. Print per-class summaries inter-class distance matrix. Default TRUE.

Use directly terra::zonal(),     terra::mask(), terra::freq(). metrics data.frame per-class pixel counts,     area fractions (%), LAB RGB means SDs, mean distance     centroid, actual mean colour rendered hex. title: Classify peat material classes from a minirhizotron RGB raster — classify_peat_rgb
text: centroids table one row per material class columns: class Character. Name class (e.g.     "dark_peat", "root"). Becomes factor level     result$map class column result$metrics. L, , B Numeric. class centroid's     coordinates CIE LAB colour space (D65 illuminant): L     lightness (0 = black, 100 = white), green-red axis     (negative = green, positive = red), B blue-yellow     axis (negative = blue, positive = yellow). *mean* LAB     values representative pixels class. MAX_DIST Numeric. per-class assignment radius,     Euclidean LAB units. pixel assigned class whose centroid     nearest LAB space, distance <=     MAX_DIST; otherwise pixel labelled "unclassified".     Larger values classify pixels risk merging visually distinct     materials; smaller values leave pixels unclassified. Typical     values roughly 10-30. Pixel RGB values converted LAB internally distances computed, L//B never raw RGB numbers.

title: Classify peat material classes from a minirhizotron RGB raster — classify_peat_rgb
text: default centroids table calibrated one specific scanner site, data derive via build_peat_centroids. build_peat_centroids() takes picks: named list one element per material class. element numeric matrix exactly 3 columns (R, G, B 0-255), every row one colour sample believed belong class. Classes may different numbers rows. function converts matrix LAB, averages single centroid, returns data.frame format .default_peat_centroids() – ready pass straight back classify_peat_rgb(centroids = ...). simplest approach read representative RGB values scan (e.g. using image viewer's colour picker) enter directly: Alternatively, picks can extracted known representative patches image. Important: build_peat_centroids() always treats pick values 0-255 (auto-scaling). raster stores values 0-1, multiply 255 building picks centroids pixels seen inside classify_peat_rgb() scale. build_peat_centroids() prints diagnostics (intra-class spread, inter-class distances, MAX_DIST coverage) help sanity-check choices. Provide class every material present scans: classification nearest-centroid, material without class snapped whichever defined class closest.

what: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: Performs three sequential cleaning operations binary segmented image: 1. **Hole filling** – fills black regions enclosed white (segmentation    gaps inside roots). 2. **Artifact removal** — removes isolated white specks connected    image border (false-positive root detections). 3. **Edge smoothing** *(optional, default)* – applies morphological    closing smooth jagged root edges.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
code: clean_image(   img,   pre_threshold = NULL,   pre_threshold_method = "global",   pre_threshold_window_size = 15,   max_hole_size = NULL,   max_artifact_size = NULL,   edge_smooth = FALSE,   kernel_shape = "disk",   kernel_size = 3,   iterations = 1,   select.layer = NULL,   output_format = "spatrast",   report = FALSE )

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: img `cimg` object, `SpatRaster`, matrix, file path. pre_threshold Numeric (0-1) `NULL` (default). `NULL`, [image_threshold()] applied `img` first, using value `threshold` argument, hole-filling artifact removal. pre_threshold_method Thresholding method passed [image_threshold()] `pre_threshold` set: `"global"` (default) `"adaptive"`. pre_threshold_window_size Window size passed [image_threshold()] `pre_threshold_method = "adaptive"`. Default `15`. max_hole_size Maximum hole size pixels fill.  `NULL`, enclosed holes filled.  See **Choosing thresholds** . max_artifact_size Maximum artifact size pixels remove.  `NULL`, isolated white regions removed. edge_smooth Logical.  Apply morphological closing hole/artifact cleaning.  Default `FALSE`. kernel_shape Structuring element shape edge smoothing: `"disk"` (default), `"square"`, `"diamond"`. kernel_size Structuring element size (odd integer).  Default `3`. iterations Number closing iterations edge smoothing. Default `1`. select.layer Integer `NULL`.  layer use multi-layer inputs. output_format Character.  Format returned object.  One `"spatrast"` (default), `"cimg"`, `"matrix"`.  Using `"spatrast"` means result can passed directly `terra::plot()`, `skeletonize_image()`, `root_length()`, etc. without conversion. report Logical.  `TRUE`, also calls [report_image_components()] *original* (uncleaned) image cleaning.  `output_format = "spatrast"` (default), cleaned raster returned directly even `report = TRUE`; report printed side effect.  Default `FALSE`.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: cleaned image format specified `output_format`   (`SpatRaster`, `cimg`, matrix).

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: `skeletonize_image()` uses Medial Axis Transform, driven distance transform.  Small holes inside root inflate local distance values force medial axis bifurcate around hole, producing spurious branching points.  Isolated artifact pixels produce phantom skeleton segments.  Cleaning first yields much cleaner skeleton.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: `edge_smooth = TRUE` applies morphological closing slightly dilates erodes root edges.  can merge closely adjacent roots alter root diameter measurements.  use segmentation output jagged edges; leave (`FALSE`, default) otherwise.

title: Detect endpoints and branching points in a skeleton image — detect_skeleton_points
text: computation proceeds follows: 1. Raster values converted numeric matrix. 2. 1-pixel zero-padding border added around matrix. 3. pixel original image:    - 3x3 window extracted padded matrix    - center pixel excluded    - Neighbor count computed sum remaining 8 values Classification rules: - Endpoint: pixel == 1 neighbor count == 1 - Branch point: pixel == 1 neighbor count > 2 Outputs converted back SpatRaster objects.

title: Zhang-Suen thinning using lookup table (LUT implementation) — lut_thin_fast
text: algorithm operates follows: 1. Raster values extracted numeric vector. 2. iteration reconstructs matrix representation image. 3. zero-padded border added around image. 4. pixel equal 1:    - 3x3 neighbourhood extracted    - weighted sum (mask encoding) produces unique neighbourhood code 5. code used index 256-entry lookup table:    - LUT value 1 3 -> pixel removed first sub-step    - LUT value 2 3 -> pixel removed second sub-step 6. Pixels updated -place vector representation 7. Iteration stops pixels removed max_iter reached Important properties implementation: - Raster written back end - intermediate steps operate -memory matrix/vector data - Neighborhood encoding performed via explicit loops image pixels

Compute 90th, 95th, 99th percentile diameter distribution per bin, conditional means quantile, threshold-based root lengths (see diameter_thresholds), modal diameter peaks via modal_peaks().  Default FALSE. calc_landscape_metrics Logical. Compute patch-level landscape metrics per depth bin via root_scape_metrics(): nearest-neighbour distance (enn_mn), joint entropy (joinent), relative mutual information (relmutinf), number patches (np), contagion (contag).  Slow: one call per depth bin per image.  Default FALSE. calc_color_metrics Logical. Compute mean chromatic coordinates (rcc, gcc, bcc), hue, saturation, luminosity, raw RGB channel means separately root pixels background pixels via tube_coloration().  Requires path.rgb.  Default FALSE. calc_root_angles Logical. Compute deep_drive (fraction skeleton pixels whose D8 flow direction matches locally optimal downward direction) mean.steepness.angle / sd.steepness.angle (degrees, 0 = horizontal, 90 = vertical). Uses deep_drive().  Default FALSE. calc_root_order_metrics Logical. Build per-image branching-order graph via branch_order_map() summarise per depth bin per tube.  Adds mean.branch_order, max.branch_order, mean.root_order, lateral_root_fraction per depth bin, plus tube-level main_root.* / lateral_roots.* columns (length, diameter, branching frequency, etc., split order_metrics(..., focal = "thickest")) n_root_orders (highest branch order found).  Requires skeleton.  Slow: builds one segment graph per image.  Default FALSE. calc_density_metrics Logical. Compute rootpx.density (percent root area cover) rootlength.density (cm root length per cm^2 imaged area) per bin.  Auto-enables calc_root_pixels calc_root_length.  Default TRUE. calc_distribution_indices Logical. Compute tube-level indices: mrd (mean rooting depth), rpi (root penetration index), total.length.density (summed length density bins, cm root per cm^2 per cm depth).  Auto-enables calc_density_metrics.  Default TRUE. calc_advanced_metrics Logical. Compute per-bin derived metrics: rootlength.fraction (bin's length density fraction tube total), mean.var.diameter (mean within-bin diameter variance), rootsurface_rootvolume_ratio (lateral surface area cylinder volume, summed skeleton pixels bin expressed cm^2 per cm^3).  Auto-enables calc_distribution_indices calc_diameter_stats.  Default TRUE. diameter_thresholds Numeric vector. Diameter cut-offs computing rootlength..* avg.diameter..* columns. Units set diameter_threshold_unit.  Default c(0.2, 0.5, 1). diameter_threshold_unit Character. Unit diameter_thresholds: "mm" (default), "cm", "px". output_path Character NULL. provided, result saved .RData file path.  exported object named root.depth.metrics.  Parent directories created exist.  Default NULL (file written). verbose Logical. Print per-image progress lines showing image index, per-image time, cumulative elapsed time, estimated remaining time, predicted clock-time completion.  Default TRUE.

title: Compute root traits over a depth profile from segmented (mini)rhizotron images — root_depth_metrics
text: data frame one row per tube x depth-bin combination.  Always   present columns: Tube, Session, Plot, depth.   columns depend metric groups enabled; disabled   failed metrics appear NA rather absent.  Returns   NULL invisibly every image failed.

title: Compute root traits over a depth profile from segmented (mini)rhizotron images — root_depth_metrics
text: Surface--volume ratio.  skeleton pixel, root segment modelled cylinder length \(l_i\) (D8 path length pixel cm) radius \(r_i\) (half local diameter cm).  lateral surface area \(2 \pi r_i l_i\) volume \(\pi r_i^2 l_i\).  ratio simplifies \(2 / r_i\). rootsurface_rootvolume_ratio length-weighted mean \(2 / r_i\) skeleton pixels depth bin, units cm\(^{-1}\) (cm^2 surface per cm^3 volume).  Thicker roots smaller ratio; fine roots larger ratio. Fault tolerance.  Every metric block wrapped tryCatch.  Failures produce [RootScanR] SKIPPED message NA values; never abort run. Dependency resolution.  Enabling higher-level metric silently enables prerequisites prints message listing auto-enabled.

title: Estimate Root Diameters — root_diameter
text: function works follows: - Converts input image binary format (`cimg`). - Applies distance transform compute Euclidean distance foreground (root) pixels. - Skeletonizes binary image identify root centerlines. - Filters distance values retain corresponding skeletonized regions. - Computes diameter statistics, including quantiles, mean, median diameters. function supports various input formats normalizes image values range [0, 1] needed. uses `terra` package raster operations `imager` package image processing.

title: Root length estimation from skeleton images — root_length
text: Let Nd number diagonal pixel connections number orthogonal pixel connections skeleton. Freeman methods treat skeleton discrete chain-code path: - freeman_basic:   L = sqrt(2) * Nd + - freeman_corrected:   L = 0.948 * (sqrt(2) * Nd + ) Kimura methods treat skeleton discretized representation underlying continuous curve reduce orientation bias: - kimura1:   L = sqrt(Nd^2 + (Nd + )^2) - kimura2 (default):   L = sqrt(Nd^2 + (Nd + /2)^2) + /2 Kimura2 estimator generally preferred due improved stability across object orientations curvature distributions.

title: Skeletonize binary image — skeletonize_image
text: Processing steps: 1. Input converted single-layer binary SpatRaster using    `load_flexible_image()`. 2. Foreground pixel count computed. 3. Skeletonization performed using `lut_thin_fast()`:    - iterative removal pixels based 3x3 neighbourhood codes    - lookup table determines pixel deletions two sub-steps per iteration 4. Output final thinned binary raster. 5. Optionally, overlay image generated:    - original image marked base layer    - skeleton pixels overlaid separate class    - saved using base R PNG plotting

text: index Numeric vector specifying positions. parameter List : lambda: shape parameter controlling steepness x0: inflection threshold parameter index.spacing Character. text: P Numeric probability vector. Q Numeric probability vector. index Numeric vector defining position (e.g., depth layers). index.spacing Character. TRUE, aligns vectors unequal length. cut Logical. TRUE, truncates longer vector instead padding.

title: Tail-weighted Jensen-Shannon divergence — tail_weighted_js_divergence
text: Jensen-Shannon divergence defined : $$JS(P,Q) = 1/2 KL(P || M) + 1/2 KL(Q || M)$$ \(M = (P + Q)/2\). Weighting applied prior normalization P, Q, M.

Reverse weighting direction. alignPQ Logical. TRUE, aligns vectors unequal length. cut Logical. TRUE, truncates longer vector instead padding.

title: Tail-weighted Kullback-Leibler divergence — tail_weighted_kl_divergence

title: Tail-weighted Kullback-Leibler divergence — tail_weighted_kl_divergence
text: KL divergence asymmetric: $$D_{KL}(P \parallel Q) = \sum P \log(P / Q)$$ Weighting modifies contribution index position prior normalization.
